# Copilot Instructions for AI-Driven Text Vectorization Agent

<!-- Use this file to provide workspace-specific custom instructions to Copilot. For more details, visit https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/copilot-customization#_use-a-githubcopilotinstructionsmd-file -->

## Project Context
This is an AI-driven text vectorization agent project implementing advanced skip-gram methodologies with novel pattern recognition algorithms (natural logarithmic and prime-number-based intervals) for semantic analysis.

## Key Architecture Principles
- **Multi-agent architecture**: Vectorization Agent, Correlation Agent, Analysis Orchestrator
- **Advanced skip-pattern algorithms**: Prime-number and logarithmic interval-based context extraction
- **Scalable vector space construction**: Multidimensional semantic analysis
- **Cloud-ready deployment**: Azure/AWS integration

## Code Generation Guidelines
- Use Python 3.10+ features and type hints throughout
- Implement comprehensive error handling and logging
- Follow PEP 8 style guidelines
- Create modular, testable components
- Include docstrings with mathematical formulations where applicable
- Prioritize performance optimization for large-scale text processing

## Key Libraries in Use
- **spaCy**: Text preprocessing and NLP pipeline
- **Gensim**: Vector modeling and Word2Vec implementations
- **NumPy/SciPy**: Mathematical operations and prime number generation
- **Scikit-learn**: Machine learning utilities and evaluation metrics
- **FastAPI**: API service implementation
- **Matplotlib/Plotly**: Data visualization

## Agent Communication Patterns
- Use async/await for inter-agent communication
- Implement RESTful APIs for stateless interactions
- Use message queues for asynchronous processing
- Shared vector databases for efficient similarity searches

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for all core algorithms
- Integration tests for agent interactions
- Performance benchmarks for scalability validation
- Semantic quality tests for vector spaces
